body.title: [sprint] Intent extractor: skip cleanly when inference unavailable (Haiku offline)
body.description:
**Problem:** The ticket-add path (cc_queue.py) calls IntentExtractorDevice.predict to decorate the `intention` field. When inference is intentionally-off (per [[project_ds_cost_constraint]], Anthropic/OpenRouter unavailable), the call fails with 'unknown model' and silently drops intention decoration. This is currently noise (error repeated per-ticket) and will undermine the intentions design ([[project_goals_to_intentions]]) once it ships — the corpus quality depends on reliable extraction.

**Proposed shape:** Detect inference-unavailable early in the extractor call path (catch the specific error or test for model availability upfront), skip inference cleanly, leave the literal `intention:` field as-is (already present in the ticket), and log *once* at debug level instead of erroring per-call. The intention field is optional; a missing inferred-intention is safe while decorating (T-test-intent-decoration tests this).

**Affected files:** devlab/claudecode/cc_queue.py (_decorate_with_intent function, line ~1700), possibly devices/classifier/cli.py (IntentExtractorDevice error path).
**Design rules:** Log at debug or skip (CLAUDE.md structural rule: log every state change and interface crossing) — the skip is an interface crossing (attempted MCP call). Don't crash the queue on inference unavailability (it's intentional).
**Scope boundary:** IN — detect inference-unavailable, skip cleanly, maintain the literal intention field. OUT — turning inference back on; changing the intention field schema; modifying the decorator for other errors (those are separate tickets).
**Completion criteria:** Ticket-add with a literal `intention:` field succeeds when Haiku/OpenRouter are unavailable; no error is emitted to stdout/stderr; a debug log notes the skip (e.g., 'skipped intent extraction: inference unavailable'); `inferred_intention` remains unset.
**Test plan:** Add a ticket with inference explicitly offline (set an env var to disable it, or mock the device unavailable); run cc_queue.py add; verify success, no error, debug log present. The existing T-test-intent-decoration suite should pass unchanged.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] datacenter_logs/<device>/<subsystem>/ is the log hierarchy. Never write to a flat — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] PATTERN-001: BaseDevice / BaseShim Lifecycle: `rollback()` must be idempotent — safe to call even if — applies: tags: pattern, pattern-001 (source: docs/design_patterns_inventory.md#PATTERN-001: BaseDevice / BaseShim Lifecycle)
[error] PATTERN-004: Flat-File Registry (Atomic Write): The skeleton must never depend on Postgres — skeleto — applies: tags: pattern, pattern-004 (source: docs/design_patterns_inventory.md#PATTERN-004: Flat-File Registry (Atomic Write))
…and 37 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
body.intention: I intend that the ticket-add path remains smooth when intent-extraction inference is unavailable (e.g., Haiku/OpenRouter offline per DS-cost-constraint), rather than failing noisily.
